solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int countCharacters(String[] words, String chars) {
        int[] charsCount = new int[26];
        for (char c : chars.toCharArray()) {
            charsCount[c - 'a']++;
        }

        int totalLength = 0;
        for (String word : words) {
            int[] wordCount = new int[26];
            for (char c : word.toCharArray()) {
                wordCount[c - 'a']++;
            }

            boolean canForm = true;
            for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
                if (wordCount[i] > charsCount[i]) {
                    canForm = false;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (canForm) {
                totalLength += word.length();
            }
        }

        return totalLength;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N + M), where N is the total number of characters in all words and M is the length of chars
space complexity: O(1) (fixed-size arrays of size 26)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses frequency counting to determine if a word can be formed from the characters in chars. It counts the frequency of each character in chars and for each word, compares its character frequency with that of chars. This approach is efficient and avoids unnecessary computations.
